zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda Crofton於1277年出生時，她的父親Clement de Crofton37歲，母親Johanna de Ulnesby32歲。她於1300年與Richard Salkeld在英國英格蘭波藍德瑟爾斯比結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1345年7月在英國英格蘭波藍德維瑟拉爾享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: Crofton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Crofton, for example in Cumbria, Greater London (formerly in Kent), Hampshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire, and Yorkshire. Most of these are named from Old English croft ‘paddock, vegetable garden’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’, but the one in Greater London probably has as its first element Old English cropp ‘swelling, mound’ (compare Cropper ) and that in Lincolnshire Old English croh ‘saffron’ (from Latin crocus).

History: A family called Crofton was established in Ireland by John Crofton (died 1610), who held high office under Elizabeth I and acquired vast estates when he accompanied Sir Henry Sidney, Lord Deputy, into Ireland in 1565.

variants.name: Cropper
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): occupational name for a picker of fruit or vegetables or a reaper of cereal crops, from cropper(e) ‘one who reaps crops or trims trees’, an agent derivative of Middle English cropt(en) ‘to pick’. The word was used also to denote the polling of livestock and the name may therefore have been given to someone with this responsibility.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda Crofton was born in 1277, her father, Clement de Crofton, was 37 and her mother, Johanna de Ulnesby, was 32. She married Richard Salkeld in 1300, in Thursby, Cumberland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died in July 1345, in Wetheral, Cumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68.

Elle a épousé Richard Salkeld en 1300, à Thursby, Cumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en juillet 1345, à Wetheral, Cumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Crofton(이)가 1277년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Clement de Crofton(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna de Ulnesby(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1300년 영국 잉글랜드 c벌랜드 스르스비에서 Richard Salkeld(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1345년 7월 영국 잉글랜드 c벌랜드 w더앨에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Salkeld в 1300 г. в местности турсби, кумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери.
